_README: REFERENCE TARGET — NOT the live settings file. This documents the trimmed canonical shape the umbrella's .claude/settings.json should converge to. The live trim is a gated, manual step tracked as a USER.TODO item; claude-settings-doctor.js only REPORTS drift, it never edits the live file. Legitimate top-level keys for the umbrella: 'permissions' (repo-relative policy), 'mcpServers' (only if umbrella-backed, with repo-relative or env-portable commands), and 'hooks' (whose commands are repo-relative, e.g. 'bash scripts/...', not '/home/<user>/...'). Hardcoded absolute user-home paths (/home/..., /Users/..., /root/..., C:\Users\) are never permitted in command strings; use $HOME/, ${HOME}/, ~/, or %APPDATA% and add an explicit allowlist entry when a home reference is unavoidable. The key env.CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS must not appear.

permissions._note: Repo-relative allow/deny rules. Example shape only; the live file's real permission set is preserved during the gated trim.

mcpServers._note: Include only umbrella-backed servers whose 'command' is repo-relative or resolved via PATH/env. Drop user-specific MCPs (those live in ~/.codex/config.toml or the user's global ~/.claude).

hooks._note: Hook commands must be repo-relative (e.g. 'bash scripts/foo.sh' or 'node scripts/foo.js'). Commands referencing /home/<user>/.claude/hooks/... are user-global config that does not belong in the committed umbrella settings. The PostToolUse/PreToolUse entries below are the umbrella's OWN repo-relative hooks (scripts/hooks/*.sh) and should survive the gated trim of the live settings.json; the user-global hooks in the live file (ruvector/ccg/gitnexus/global/*) are drift to be dropped.
